summary: Optimize agent loops, card packs, MCP session lifecycles, tool-call count, and multi-agent runtime patterns with measurement.

summary: Configure and review filesystem MCP access with a path-boundary checklist, method allowlists, and enforced out-of-root request refusal.

summary: Query, ingest, maintain, and troubleshoot local RAG MCP corpora for semantic document retrieval while protecting sensitive data.

name: review-comment-style-mining

name: agent-evaluation-lab
summary: Prompt, skill, red-team, and agent behavior evaluation workflows.

name: context7
summary: Fetch up-to-date library and framework documentation via the Context7 MCP server.

mcp.warning: Makes remote HTTP requests to retrieve documentation. Optional: set CONTEXT7_API_KEY for higher rate limits.

name: sequential-thinking
summary: Enable structured, multi-step reasoning chains via the MCP sequential-thinking server.

mcp.warning: Low risk. No filesystem or remote write access. Safe for most environments.

name: playwright
summary: Browser automation via Microsoft Playwright MCP server. Runs headless by default.

mcp.warning: High browser-automation risk. Can visit arbitrary URLs, download files, and interact with web UIs. Runs headless by default; remove --headless for headed mode. Restrict allowed origins in production environments.

name: filesystem-repo-scoped
summary: Filesystem MCP server scoped to the repository root. Requires replacing the ${REPO_ROOT} placeholder before use.

mcp.warning: High local file risk. Grants the agent full read/write access to all files under the scoped path. Replace ${REPO_ROOT} with the absolute path of the repository root. Never scope to the home directory.

name: memory
summary: Persistent local memory via the MCP memory server. Stores and retrieves facts across sessions.

mcp.warning: Persists conversation context to a local JSON file. Do not store secrets, credentials, or personal data in memory. Review the memory file location before use in shared environments.

name: destructive-bash-guard
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for blocking hard-to-reverse Bash commands like rm -rf and git reset --hard.

name: mcp-write-guard
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for intercepting MCP write-class tool calls and requiring explicit approval.

name: mcp-config-change-approval
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for requiring approval before any MCP server configuration file is modified.

name: env-file-mutation-approval
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for blocking writes to .env files without explicit approval.

name: generated-file-warning
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for warning when an agent attempts to edit a generated file.

name: console-log-check
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for catching debug statements in staged changes before a commit.

name: large-diff-warning
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for warning on oversized commits and prompting to split or confirm.

name: session-log
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for appending a timestamped audit trail of agent tool calls to a local log file.

name: release-readiness-protocol
summary: Check version bump, changelog, compare range, package metadata, validation commands, publish steps, and rollback plan before cutting a release.

name: changelog-generator
summary: Turn commits, PRs, or diffs into user-facing release notes or an internal changelog, with modes for breaking-change scanning.

name: build-fix-minimal-diff
summary: Fix broken builds, type errors, or test failures with the smallest safe diff â€” no opportunistic refactors, no cleanup.

name: environment-doctor
summary: Diagnose local agent and project setup problems â€” missing commands, broken MCP configs, stale generated assets, bad paths, and missing env vars.

name: webapp-visual-testing
summary: Inspect and test web UIs using screenshots, browser automation, accessibility snapshots, and console/network signals.

name: agent-readable-docs
summary: Use when writing technical documentation that needs to be readable by both humans and AI models, converting existing docs to HADS format, validating a HADS document, or optimizing documentation for token-efficient AI consumption.

name: ai-regression-testing
summary: Deterministic checks first, agent review second, regression test for every real bug fixed or document why not. Targets the blind spot where an agent writes and reviews its own code.

name: api-doc-review
summary: Verify that API endpoints match their OpenAPI/Swagger specifications.

name: architecture-decision-records
summary: Record why an architectural choice was made to prevent agents or humans from unintentionally reverting it.

name: architecture-simplification
summary: Use to collapse over-engineered abstractions, remove unnecessary layers, or consolidate redundant logic.

name: baseline-comparison-review
summary: Ensure that new complex models actually outperform simple, naive baselines.

name: canonical-advisor-routing
summary: Process-first advisor routing with artifact capture

name: change-impact-check
summary: Use before submitting a PR or considering a task done to evaluate the 'blast radius' of your changes.

name: ci-failure-readout
summary: Use when a CI pipeline fails to extract the actual error from thousands of lines of logs.

name: context-docs
summary: Maintain short, focused Markdown files per subsystem to provide agents with isolated context.

name: context-minimization
summary: Use continuously during long tasks. Teaches how to read less, output less, and keep the LLM context window lean and fast.

name: dataset-split-review
summary: Audit the methodology used to split data into train, validation, and test sets.

name: dead-code-removal
summary: Use to identify and safely delete unused functions, classes, exports, and files.

name: dependency-cleanup
summary: Use to audit and remove unused or redundant third-party dependencies from package manifests.

name: doc-consistency-check
summary: Audit documentation for broken file paths, outdated commands, and renamed variables.

name: experiment-tracking-review
summary: Verify that all hyperparameters, metrics, and data references are properly logged.

name: failure-triage
summary: Use when confronted with an unknown failure in CI or production to rapidly categorize the issue before deep debugging.

name: flaky-test-investigation
summary: Use to diagnose tests that pass and fail intermittently without code changes.

name: handoff-discipline
summary: Use when completing a task or running out of context limit. Ensures the next session or human engineer has exactly what they need to resume work instantly.

name: handoff-documentation
summary: Write state-restoration documents for passing tasks between agents or engineers.

name: incident-readout
summary: Use after fixing a bug to generate a blameless post-mortem summary for human review.

name: incremental-migration
summary: Use when migrating APIs, libraries, or patterns across a large codebase. Ensures safe, step-by-step progress rather than risky mega-commits.

name: log-driven-diagnosis
summary: Use when debugging complex runtime failures, distributed systems, or issues where a local debugger cannot be attached.

name: memory-build-workflow
summary: Use when a user needs to build or refresh persistent graph memory from a mixed corpus and the right path may include graphify, incremental update, or helper conversion before ingestion.

name: memory-optimization-workflow
summary: Use when deciding the lowest-cost context path for a mixed corpus, especially when choosing among direct reading, helper conversion, graph build, graph update, or graph query.

name: memory-query-workflow
summary: Use when a graph already exists and the user needs retrieval, tracing, explanation, or gap detection from graph memory before reopening the full corpus.

name: minimal-reproduction
summary: Use to isolate a bug from a large application into a standalone, runnable script or single test case.

name: ml-leakage-check
summary: Identify and prevent target leakage in ML preprocessing pipelines.

name: model-evaluation-reporting
summary: Standardize the reporting of model metrics to ensure statistical rigor and business relevance.

name: naming-and-structure-cleanup
summary: Use to enforce consistent naming conventions and file structures across a project without changing business logic.

name: pre-release-verification
summary: Use before tagging a release or deploying to production to ensure all quality gates have passed.

name: readme-hardening
summary: Ensure the project README provides immediate, exact commands for setup, testing, and deployment to help agents and humans bootstrap quickly.

name: regression-bisecting
summary: Use when a bug was recently introduced but you don't know which commit caused it.

name: reproducible-training-runs
summary: Analyzes ML training scripts to enforce seed setting, deterministic operations, and environment tracking for exact reproducibility.

name: risk-based-review
summary: Use when reviewing code (or your own plan) to allocate attention based on the danger of the change.

name: semantic-layer-change-review
summary: Use when modifying dbt metrics or semantic models to ensure mathematical correctness and backwards compatibility.

name: strategic-context-compaction
summary: Compact context at logical phase boundaries - after research, after planning, after debugging - rather than mid-task. Preserves useful state while clearing noise.

name: task-intake
summary: Use at the beginning of a new task. Ensures you fully understand the requirements, boundaries, and acceptance criteria before writing code.

name: test-preserving-refactor
summary: Use to restructure code while guaranteeing that all existing tests continue to pass.

name: training-pipeline-debugging
summary: Diagnose NaN losses, out-of-memory errors, and shape mismatches in deep learning or ML pipelines.

name: tri-model-review
summary: Multi-model orchestration - route to two external advisors, then synthesize

name: release
summary: Run a release-readiness checklist and produce a go/no-go readout before tagging or publishing.

name: changelog
summary: Generate user-facing release notes or a changelog entry from commits, PRs, or a diff.

name: build-fix
summary: Fix a failing build, type check, or test suite with the smallest safe diff.

name: doctor
summary: Diagnose environment, tooling, and agent setup problems with a structured report.

name: visual-review
summary: Inspect a web UI with browser evidence: screenshots, accessibility, console/network signals, structured findings.

name: mcp-check
summary: Check MCP server prerequisites and configuration before activating or using a server.

name: mcp-smoke
summary: Test that an MCP server starts and responds before adding it to an agent configuration.

name: fetch
summary: Generic web/HTTP fetch and content intake via the MCP fetch server.

mcp.warning: Fetches arbitrary remote URLs. No credentials by default. Treat returned content as untrusted data.

name: time
summary: Timezone and time conversion helper via the MCP time server.

mcp.warning: Low risk. No filesystem access, no network write, no credentials required.

name: git-local
summary: Local repository git status, diff, and log via mcp-server-git. Scoped to the repository root.

mcp.warning: Replace ${REPO_ROOT} with the absolute path of the repository root before use. Git tools can mutate local repository state â€” review enabled operations before activating write capabilities.

name: postgres-readonly
summary: Read-oriented Postgres access for local/dev analytics. Requires a SELECT-only connection string.

mcp.warning: Set POSTGRES_READONLY_DSN to a SELECT-only connection string. Never use production write credentials, a superuser role, or a role with DDL permissions. The MCP server executes queries on behalf of the agent â€” scope access strictly.

name: github-remote
summary: Remote HTTP GitHub MCP endpoint. Alternative to github-local that avoids Docker. Requires a narrow-scope PAT.

mcp.warning: Uses the hosted GitHub MCP endpoint. Set GITHUB_MCP_PAT to a narrow-scope PAT â€” prefer read-only scopes unless write is explicitly required. Token is sent in every request header. Codex TOML HTTP-type format may require verification against current Codex documentation.

name: cloud-cli-guard
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for blocking destructive cloud CLI commands and allowing read-only/plan commands through.

name: migration-review-required
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for surfacing a safety checklist when database migration files are written or edited.

name: todo-fixme-blocker
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for catching bare TODO/FIXME/HACK markers in new code before completion or commit.

name: session-compaction-helper
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for saving critical task state before context compaction and restoring it afterward.

name: build-analysis-post
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for summarizing build/test/lint output and extracting actionable failures after tool execution.

name: design-quality-check
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for prompting visual review when frontend UI files are written or edited.

name: dev-server-tmux-guard
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for redirecting long-running dev server commands to background or tmux to avoid blocking the agent session.

name: supabase
summary: Supabase project MCP integration for database, auth, and management API access.

mcp.warning: DRAFT: Verify exact flags against current @supabase/mcp-server-supabase docs. SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN grants management API access. Use a project-scoped token. Never use a service role key or admin token in agent workflows.

name: vercel
summary: Vercel project and deployment access via remote HTTP MCP endpoint.

mcp.warning: DRAFT: Verify SSE endpoint URL against current Vercel MCP docs. VERCEL_TOKEN grants access to Vercel projects and deployments. Use a narrowly scoped token. Deploy/promote/delete actions require explicit approval.

name: cloudflare-docs
summary: Cloudflare documentation retrieval via official read-only remote MCP endpoint.

mcp.warning: DRAFT: Verify SSE endpoint URL against current Cloudflare MCP documentation. No authentication required for read-only documentation access.

name: exa-search
summary: Exa semantic search and research integration for agent workflows.

mcp.warning: EXA_API_KEY required. Queries are sent to Exa remote search API. Avoid sending proprietary queries or sensitive code fragments via search.

name: atlassian
summary: Atlassian Jira and Confluence MCP integration for ticket and wiki access.

mcp.warning: DRAFT: Verify package name and env vars against current @atlassian/mcp-server docs. ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN grants access to Jira and Confluence projects. Use read-only scopes where possible.

name: browserbase
summary: Cloud browser automation via Browserbase MCP for remote web interaction.

mcp.warning: Browserbase sessions can interact with live authenticated web apps. Treat every session as if it can access real user accounts and production services. BROWSERBASE_API_KEY and BROWSERBASE_PROJECT_ID required.

name: e2b-sandbox
summary: Remote sandboxed code execution via E2B MCP for safe agent-driven code runs.

mcp.warning: E2B executes code in remote sandboxes. E2B_API_KEY required. Do not send secrets, production credentials, or proprietary code without policy approval.

name: pre-commit-quality-check
summary: Aggregator recipe that runs no-secrets-preflight, large-diff-warning, console-log-check, and todo-fixme-blocker before git commit.

name: doc-file-warning
summary: Warn when agents write Markdown or text files outside approved documentation locations.

name: quality-gate-after-edit
summary: After any source file edit, suggest the narrowest relevant validation command for that language.

name: typescript-post-edit-check
summary: After TypeScript or TSX edits, suggest a focused tsc --noEmit or project typecheck command.

name: handoff-completeness-check
summary: Before session stop, verify the handoff covers what changed, validation run, failures, and next steps.

name: mcp-elicitation-guard
summary: Block or require explicit approval for MCP elicitation requests that ask for secrets, credentials, or broad permissions.

name: session-lifecycle-state
summary: Save a compact state snapshot at session end and restore it at session start for cross-session continuity.
